# The busy-queue black hole ŌĆö why talking to the dock mid-turn feels broken

**Date:** 2026-07-13 ┬Ę **Method:** live acoustic trials (laptop speaker ŌåÆ dock-redmi mic)
driven remotely (`debug/say`, `debug/event`), judged from station ground truth only
(`/debug/addressed` ring with utterance windows, `/api/observability` turn timings,
`/tmp/orbit-dev.log` `[conv]` transitions, `.data/enrich-audio` WAVs). Follows the
stress-dock-pipeline method: instrument, many reps, honest failure classes.

## TL;DR

1. **Any speech while the dock is thinking/speaking is unanswerable by construction.**
   The busy-queue drain fires at TURN END, when the mode is still `thinking` (phone TTS
   always starts after turn-end in every observed trace) ŌĆö so the drained batch re-enters
   `onAddressedFinal`, sees `pre.mode=thinking`, and is re-queued with a **reset
   `firstAt`**. It bounces from turn-end to turn-end until a drain happens >20s after the
   last bounce, then the whole batch is dropped **with no trace** (the staleness-drop
   path doesn't `trace()`). 0/6 queued utterances got answered across trials; the code
   admits no path that could answer one.
2. Follow-ups that DO get answered bypass the queue entirely: their STT final happens to
   land during the 8s `followup` window (STT finalization lags speech end by 1ŌĆō3.5s).
   Whether your follow-up lives or dies is a race between STT lag and the reply's
   LLM+TTS timeline ŌĆö invisible and nondeterministic to the user. That IS the
   "sometimes takes it, sometimes queues it up" experience.
3. **Ghost contamination makes it worse:** wake+command / wake / task / self turns never
   drain the queue at all (`.then(drain)` only exists on the plain RAN-TURN branch,
   `modules/brain/index.ts:660`). An orphaned item's old `firstAt` poisons the batch, so
   the NEXT fresh utterance queued with it is silently dropped too (observed: "Please
   also tell me what color grass is", 7s old, dropped because a 84s-old ghost shared the
   batch).
4. **Baseline latency is the amplifier:** median 8.0s / p90 13s from turn start ŌåÆ reply
   audio (39 real user turns, session s-yv9e). Root: nearly every turn runs TWO serial
   LLM steps ŌĆö `set_face` tool call (~3.5ŌĆō6.6s) then the text step ŌĆö on a ~23k-token
   prompt (median; gemini-2.5-flash ttft 5.5ŌĆō8.8s each). 8s of dead air is precisely
   when a human repeats themselves ŌĆö feeding the black hole above.

## Trial evidence (all on live dock-redmi, session s-yv9e)

| # | Scenario | Queued decision | Outcome |
|---|---|---|---|
| B1 | speak during `thinking` of a wake+command turn | `queue:busy` | never ran (wake path has no drain) ŌĆö ghost |
| B2 | speak during `thinking` of a RAN-TURN turn | `queue:busy` | silently dropped at drain: batch `firstAt` poisoned by B1's ghost (84s > 20s cap), no trace |
| B3 | speak during `speaking` (TTS) | `queue:busy` | drain fired before STT final landed ŌåÆ ghost; bounced through two later turn-ends (`queue:busy` re-traces at exactly the turns' `endedAt`), then silently dropped 228s stale |
| B4 | speak during `thinking` | `queue:busy` | ANSWERED 16s later ŌĆö but via the followup window (STT final landed post-reply), not the queue |
| D1 | "Actually never mind, stop moving" during a `move` tool turn | `queue:busy` | robot kept moving; utterance dropped with the stale batch. Voice cannot stop a motion turn (only tap, or "hey orbit ŌĆ”" wake-supersede) |
| D1b | TAP during reply TTS | ŌĆö | WORKS: speakingŌåÆlistening instantly, reply aborted (`tap-interrupt`) |

Supporting single observations:

- **Early window close (the "listening but ignored" class):** tap opened an 8s window;
  a phone VAD-end shrank it to a 1.5s endpoint mid-sentence (`listening->idle
  (window-timeout)` 3.7s in); the STT utterance STARTED 400ms after the close, so the
  started-while-open rescue missed. Only the wake word in the sentence rescued the turn
  (`wake+command` at `mode=idle`).
- **Wake ack is a 6ŌĆō7s LLM turn** for a constant string (trigger text: `[wake] ŌĆ” Reply
  with EXACTLY: "did you call me?"` ŌĆö step ms 5.5ŌĆō6.6s, ~20k input tokens billed per
  "did you call me?").
- **Phantom wake:** a `[wake]` task turn ran with NO addressed-ring entry at all
  (turn-069400dc, 15s after a tap-interrupt) ŌĆö some wake trigger path bypasses the
  addressed trace; instrument before theorizing.
- **`session/end` is undone by presence:** `session s-yv9e closed (console)` was
  followed ~2s later by `presence resumed session s-yv9e` (49 msgs). A fresh session is
  impossible while the phone is connected; the 23k-token prompt keeps compounding.
- **Wake mishears die silently:** real utterance "Hey all, give me a demo of what you
  can do." (almost certainly "Hey orbitŌĆ”") ŌåÆ no wake, `skip:not-addressed`, no near-miss
  log ("all" isn't an alias), no user feedback whatsoever.

## Fix directions (ranked by UX value)

1. **Drain on `followup`, not turn-end** ŌĆö hook the drain to the speakingŌåÆfollowup
   transition (`noteSpeech(false)` / `speakEnd`), and let the drained batch bypass the
   busy check (it is by definition addressed speech from the just-finished exchange).
   With that, a queued follow-up runs the moment the dock stops talking ŌĆö the designed
   behavior, which currently never happens.
2. **Every turn kind must drain** ŌĆö wake+command, wake, task/self turns (attach the
   drain to the lane going quiet, not to one branch's `.then`). Kills the ghost class.
3. **Per-item staleness, no `firstAt` reset** ŌĆö drop only items older than the cap, not
   the batch; keep the original per-item timestamps across bounces; **trace the drop**
   (`skip:stale`) so nothing dies invisibly.
4. **Cut the serial `set_face` step** ŌĆö emotion should not cost a full extra LLM
   roundtrip before the reply. Options: same-step forcing, parallel call, or a cheap
   classifier. Halves perceived latency immediately.
5. **Trim the prompt** (23k tokens median) and/or **short-circuit the wake ack** to a
   canned utterance (no LLM) ŌĆö wake response goes from ~7s to <1s.
6. **Voice stop for motion turns** ŌĆö "stop" during an active `move` should abort motion
   (small always-on grammar, or at least document that "hey orbit, stop" supersedes).
7. **VAD-end must not close a window under an in-flight utterance** ŌĆö the STT segmenter
   had speech open when the endpoint shrink fired; gate the shrink on "no utterance in
   flight", or widen the started-while-open rescue past the endpoint tail.
8. **`session/end` should mark do-not-resume** so a console close survives presence.
9. **Feedback on silent ignores** ŌĆö near-miss instrumentation should cover more
   renderings (e.g. leading "Hey X" patterns when idle), and consider a subtle visual
   cue when an utterance was heard-but-not-addressed, so "ignored" is at least legible.

## Repro

Scripts in the session scratchpad (`monitor.py`, `driver.sh`, `midturn.sh`) ŌĆö pattern:
`debug/say` (or acoustic wake) for turn A; laptop `say` for line B during
thinking/speaking; judge from the addressed ring's `startedAt..endedAt` windows (a
re-delivered window identical to an old one = the drain bouncing a ghost, not new
speech). The addressed ring plus obs `endedAt` timestamps are sufficient to see every
mechanism above; no phone-side instrumentation needed.

## Addendum ŌĆö 2026-07-13: solutions per problem (grounded in the body/face code)

> **Why this is here.** A re-read of the actual body + face path (prompted by a
> brainŌåÆbody-coordination question) turned up a **factual error in the TL;DR above**
> (point 4) worth correcting on the record, and let me tie concrete, code-grounded fixes
> to the specific problems this RCA already named. Everything below cites the code it
> lands in ŌĆö no invented options. Each fix is stated as **problem ŌåÆ concrete lever that
> exists in this codebase today**.

### Correction to the record: `set_face` is an emotion GRAPHIC, not a body-motion step

The TL;DR (point 4) calls the pre-reply cost "a `set_face` tool call" as if the **body
gesture** is what's expensive. That's wrong. Reading the tool (`modules/brain/tools.ts:404-416`):

- `set_face` is primarily a **screen emotion** ŌĆö a phone RPC to the `face` capability that
  changes the animated face UI. The tool **awaits the face RPC ack** (`rpc.call` ŌåÆ
  `ack.isError`), so the face round-trip *is* on the critical path.
- The body gesture is a **best-effort side-effect**: `deps.motion.playGesture(...)`
  (`tools.ts:409`) is **fire-and-forget** (`playGesture` returns `void`, dispatches async ŌĆö
  `modules/bodylink/motion.ts:241-246`), **not awaited**, **not** the latency cost. Offline
  body or a higher-priority lease ŌåÆ silent no-op, face still changes.

So the pre-reply cost decomposes as **(a) a full extra LLM step** ŌĆö the model *deciding* to
emit `set_face`, on the ~23k-token prompt (dominant term; ttft 5.5ŌĆō8.8s per step per this
RCA's own numbers) **+ (b) one phone face-RPC round-trip it blocks on**. The servos are
free. Fix #4's conclusion holds; its *framing* as a motion cost does not ŌĆö the thing to cut
is the **LLM's involvement in choosing the emotion** and the **awaited screen RPC**, nothing
about the body.

### Problem ŌåÆ concrete lever (all grounded in existing code)

**P1 ŌĆö 8s reply latency, dominated by the serial `set_face` LLM step (TL;DR #4).**
Lever, using surfaces that already exist:
- **Fold the mood into the text step instead of a second tool call.** `FACES` is a fixed
  enum (`schemas.ts:60-70`); the reply and the mood can come out of ONE model step (mood as
  a response field the same step emits) rather than a separate `set_face` roundtrip. Removes
  the second serial ttft ŌĆö the direct cause of the 8s median. The prompt currently *teaches*
  the model to treat face as a deliberate separate tool call (`prompt.ts:24-28`); that
  instruction is what to change.
- **Stop awaiting the face RPC ack.** `set_face` blocks on `rpc.call` (`tools.ts:410-414`),
  but the face graphic is best-effort UX (same as the fire-and-forget gesture on line 409).
  Fire it and don't block the reply on the phone round-trip; surface any error to a log.

**P2 ŌĆö the body/face feels dead or same-y (expressive quality ŌĆö the coordination the
question was really about).** This is a **target-generation** gap, not a pacing gap: the
executor's pacing/feel machinery is already strong (`#scaledDuration`, `#effectiveDuration`,
velocity cap, smoothstep, lease ŌĆö `motion.ts:339-378`), but the *targets* are **static
keyframe clips** ŌĆö `playGesture` plays a fixed `MoveStep[]` from config (`motion.ts:241`), so
every "happy" is byte-identical and idle is silent. Concrete levers on the executor that
exists:
- **A base idle oscillator** on `neck`/`foot` (low-freq sub-degree drift) so the body is
  never dead-still. There's a natural home: `#heartbeatSweep` already ticks at 100ms
  (`motion.ts:433`) re-sending `m.targets`; an idle drift is a small offset applied there
  when no sequence is running.
- **Blend gestures onto the idle base instead of superseding.** Today a new sequence cancels
  the prior one (`#runSequence`, `motion.ts:306-309`); layering (add the gesture delta onto
  the running idle) is what reads as "alive."
- **An `intensity` scalar (0ŌĆō1) on gesture defs** driving amplitude/speed, so "mildly
  amused" vs "delighted" scale ONE gesture rather than needing N canned clips. `MoveStep`
  already carries per-step `duration_ms`/`snap`; intensity is a multiplier over the authored
  degrees + duration, applied in `#resolveSteps`/`#runSequence`.
None of this needs a new model or new hardware ŌĆö it's the missing target-generation layer on
top of the arbitration layer that's already built.

**P3 ŌĆö the idle-moods task already exists but is clip-based.** `src/tasks/packaged/idle-moods`
and the `mood-line-bench.mts` bench are the existing seam for "what does it do when idle";
the oscillator/intensity work above is the upgrade path *for that seam*, not a new subsystem.
(Left as a pointer ŌĆö audit before building on it.)

> **Scope note:** these target the *dock's 2-DOF expressive head* (`foot` pan + `neck` tilt,
> `motion.ts:280-289`) ŌĆö a small, fully-authorable action space where hand-authored keyframes
> + procedural layering are the right tool. Anything learning-based over a large continuous
> action space belongs to a *future rover arm/gripper*, a different body and a separate skill,
> not this path.

## Addendum 2 ŌĆö 2026-07-13: full code verification of the RCA + what a fix must actually change

> **Why this is here.** A line-by-line re-read of the busy-queue path
> (`modules/brain/index.ts`, `session.ts`, `conversation-state.ts`) to validate this doc
> before acting on it. **Verdict: every mechanism claim in the TL;DR is true in the code as
> written** ŌĆö and TL;DR #1 is actually *understated*: there is a SECOND, independent kill
> mechanism the RCA didn't name, which means fix direction #1 above, as stated, would NOT
> fix the black hole. Verification table first, then the correction, then the fixes that
> survive 100%-confidence scrutiny (each names the exact seam it lands in). Items that need
> design work rather than a patch are flagged, not hand-waved.

### Claim-by-claim verification (file:line, current main @ 631a07e)

| Doc claim | Verdict | Where in code |
|---|---|---|
| Busy speech is queued with `firstAt` **reset to now** on every re-queue | Ō£ģ | `index.ts:647` ŌĆö the re-entered batch takes the `else` branch (`pendingBusy.delete` ran first), minting `firstAt: Date.now()` |
| Drain fires at **turn end**, while mode is still thinking/speaking | Ō£ģ structurally, not just observed | drain is `.then()` of `handleTurnRequest` (`index.ts:660`), which resolves when the agent **loop** finishes; `speaking` only starts when the phone's tts-start marker arrives (`session.ts:545-551` ŌåÆ `speakStart`). The drained batch re-enters `onAddressedFinal`, hits `pre.mode` thinking/speaking (`index.ts:644`), re-queues |
| Stale batch dropped **with no trace** | Ō£ģ | `index.ts:667-678` ŌĆö `pendingBusy.delete` unconditionally; the `Date.now() - q.firstAt > 20s` case simply falls through, no `trace()` call |
| Only the plain RAN-TURN branch drains; wake+command / wake / task / self turns never do | Ō£ģ | wake+command runs `handleTurnRequest` with only `.catch` (`index.ts:602-608`); bare wake goes through `session.wake()` ŌåÆ the autonomous queue; task/self turns run in `#drainAuto` (`session.ts:471-518`) ŌĆö `pendingBusy` is module-local to `index.ts`, the session can't touch it |
| Follow-ups that work go via the 8s followup window, not the queue | Ō£ģ | `FOLLOWUP_MS` 8s (`conversation-state.ts:32`); `speakEnd` opens it (`:219-226`); a fresh final landing in it passes `utteranceEnded` normally |
| Wake ack is a full LLM turn for a constant string | Ō£ģ | `session.ts:355-362` ŌĆö `wake()` enqueues an autonomous turn: `Reply with EXACTLY: "${prompt}"` |
| `session/end` undone by presence | Ō£ģ | `resumableOnPresence` (`session.ts:150-153`) checks only `closedAt != null` + idle-window recency ŌĆö a console close is indistinguishable from an idle close, so the next presence signal reopens it |
| Early window close: VAD-end shrink + missed rescue | Ō£ģ | vad-end shrinks a held window to `now+1.5s` (`conversation-state.ts:247-250`); at expiry `#prune` **clamps** `#lastWindowUntil` down to the shrunk expiry (`:341`), so an utterance starting 400ms later fails the started-while-open interval (`:284-286`) |
| Two serial LLM steps per turn (`set_face` then text) | Ō£ģ mechanism | pi tool-call loop: `set_face` is a separate step and **awaits** the face RPC ack (`tools.ts:404-416`); the prompt teaches it as a deliberate tool call (`prompt.ts:24-28`). Sentence streaming (`sentence.ts`) already ships speech as it generates ŌĆö so ttft of the *pre-reply tool step* is the real dead-air term, exactly as measured |

The 23k-token median prompt and the 8.0s/13s latency numbers are live measurements ŌĆö not
re-derivable statically, but nothing in the code contradicts them and the two-step mechanism
that produces them is confirmed above.

### Correction that changes the fix: the queue has TWO kill mechanisms, and fix #1 as written only removes one

Fix direction #1 above says: drain on the speakingŌåÆfollowup transition and "let the drained
batch bypass the **busy check**". Not enough. The drain re-enters `onAddressedFinal`, and the
re-entered batch carries the **newest item's original timestamps** (`{...last}`,
`index.ts:672-675`). Even at a perfectly-timed followup drain it then faces
`utteranceEnded` (`conversation-state.ts:269-295`), which rejects it twice over:

- **Grace check:** `endedAt < now - GRACE_MS` (2.5s, `:288`) ŌåÆ not addressed. A queued
  utterance is by construction several seconds old by TTS-end (median turn 8s).
- **Started-while-open rescue can't save it:** the followup window's `#windowOpenedAt` is
  TTS-end; the queued utterance started during *thinking*, i.e. before the window opened,
  so `startedAt >= #windowOpenedAt - GRACE` (`:285`) fails too.

So the batch would trace `skip:not-addressed` ŌĆö and, worse, `markSpeechAddressed(ŌĆ”, false)`
(`index.ts:577-578`) would mis-stamp genuinely addressed speech as overheard in the recall
record. **A working fix must not re-enter `onAddressedFinal` at all.** The queue already IS
the addressed decision (only `queue:busy` items enter it); re-checking addressedness later
against a window that didn't exist when the words were spoken is the design error.

### The fix that survives scrutiny (concrete, all seams exist today)

1. **Drain = call `handleTurnRequest` directly** with the joined text (the wake+command
   branch at `index.ts:601-608` is the exact existing pattern: `tapOpen()` +
   `handleTurnRequest`, no window re-check). Apply per-item staleness (each item keeps its
   own `endedAt`; drop only items older than the cap) and `trace('drain:ran'/'skip:stale')`
   per item so nothing dies invisibly.
2. **Hook it to the settle point, not one branch's `.then`.** The single spot that means
   "the dock actually finished talking" already exists: `noteSpeech(false)` when no turn is
   active ŌĆö the `TurnSettled` marker (`session.ts:553`). A drain callback there (session ŌåÆ
   brain module, or move `pendingBusy` into the session next to `#autoQueue`, which already
   models exactly this shape) covers RAN-TURN, wake, wake+command, task and self turns in
   one place ŌĆö kills the ghost class (#2) and the bounce (#1/#3) simultaneously.
3. **Reflex stop at queue time** (fixes D1 concretely): at the moment an utterance is
   queued (`index.ts:644-649`) its text is in hand and the turn is still running. A
   deterministic stop-intent check ("stop", "never mind", "wait") ŌåÆ `session.cancel()`
   (`session.ts:522-530`), which already does `motion.stop` + agent abort + the phone
   `cancelled` frame ŌĆö the same machinery the tap uses. STT lag means it fires ~1-3s into
   the action; with an 8s median turn that is still a win, and it's the only voice path
   that can stop a `move` today.
4. **Corrections come for free** once 1+2 land: "ŌĆ”actually never mind, blue one" spoken
   mid-turn joins the batch and runs as one combined turn at settle ŌĆö the append-all
   design (`index.ts:540-546`) finally executing as documented.
5. **Canned wake ack, no LLM:** `wake()` already opens the window itself (`tapOpen`,
   `session.ts:356`); the spoken ack needs only the existing frame envelope ŌĆö
   `turn-status(thinking)` ŌåÆ `speak` ŌåÆ `turn-status(done)` (`session.ts:916,1302,1056`) ŌĆö
   with a minted turnId and no agent run. ~7s ŌåÆ sub-second, and it stops billing ~20k
   tokens per "did you call me?".
6. **`session/end` marks do-not-resume:** `SessionMeta` gains a `closedBy: 'console'`
   (or `noResume`) flag written by `store.close`; `resumableOnPresence`
   (`session.ts:150-153`) checks it. Two-line semantic fix; the pure function is already
   unit-tested in isolation.
7. **`set_face` fold-in + un-await the face ack** ŌĆö Addendum 1 P1 stands as written;
   re-verified this pass (`tools.ts:404-416`, `prompt.ts:24-28`). This is the latency fix;
   everything above is the interruptibility fix.

### Flagged ŌĆö real problems, but a patch here would be cooked up

- **Prompt at 23k tokens:** the composition is verifiable (`buildSystemPrompt`,
  `prompt.ts:84-111`: system+persona+ego+memory+skills+grounding + session transcript) but
  *which term dominates* is not ŌĆö measure the breakdown from the obs step records before
  trimming anything.
- **True voice barge-in** (talk over the dock's TTS without tap/wake): the mic hears the
  dock's own speaker, so acting on speech during `speaking` needs self-echo rejection
  first ŌĆö the busy queue's very reason for existing (`index.ts:634-643`, the dock
  interrupted itself with room audio). Design work, not a gate flip.
- **VAD-endpoint vs in-flight utterance (#7):** the station does have a usable signal
  (STT interims, `index.ts:693`) to hold the endpoint open while an utterance is mid-flight,
  but the interim gate is itself listening-state-dependent ŌĆö needs a live trial to confirm
  interims flow through the endpoint tail before building on them.
- **Standing spoken instructions** ("only respond when I say X", "keep following me"):
  the shape exists in fragments ŌĆö `wakeCfg` is a conductor-set standing respond-rule
  (`index.ts:589`), the attention gate raises thoughts, behaviours like faceFollow run as
  standing tasks ŌĆö but a general "user utterance ŌćÆ persistent policy the addressed-decision
  consults" is a new subsystem crossing the gate, conversation state and session persistence.
  Needs a design doc, not a patch.
- **Concurrent tool-talk** (aware of parallel tool progress while conversing): background
  tasks + the ~1 Hz digest already exist (`docs/tasks.md`); *in-turn* parallel tool calls
  with progress awareness is a pi-agent-loop capability question ŌĆö upstream surface, flag
  for the selfmod/extension track.

---

## Addendum 3 ŌĆö 2026-07-13: execution plan (work items, test plans, sign-off)

> Executes Addendum 2's confirmed fixes as five work items plus one harness item.
> Ordering: **WI-0 ŌåÆ WI-1 ŌåÆ WI-2** (they share the harness and the queue seam);
> **WI-3** is independent and can run in parallel; **WI-4** needs WI-0's harness but not
> the queue work; **WI-5** is independent and smallest. Nothing here touches the flagged
> items above ŌĆö those stay design work.

### Terminology (pinned, because the doc has blurred these)

Three different things get called "task"-ish in this doc; the plan below uses them
precisely:

- **Tool** ŌĆö an in-turn LLM tool call inside the agent loop (`set_face`, `move`,
  `run_task`ŌĆ”). Lives and dies within one turn.
- **Task** ŌĆö a background job running as its **own OS process**, connected back over the
  WS `tasks` topic (docs/tasks.md). Cross-turn by design. `run_task` (a *tool*) starts one.
- **Turn trigger kinds** ŌĆö `trigger.kind` on a turn request: `'user'` (addressed speech),
  `'task'` (an autonomous turn injected by task signals ŌĆö tasks.md ┬¦5), `'self'` (a
  gate-raised thought). **Overload to note:** the wake ack is an autonomous turn *labeled*
  `kind:'task'` with no task process behind it (`session.ts:359`) ŌĆö "task turn" in the
  trial table means "autonomous-queue turn", not "a running task". WI-4 removes this
  pseudo-'task' turn entirely.

---

### WI-0 ŌĆö Headless mid-turn conversation harness

> **STATUS: BUILT + SIGNED OFF ŌĆö 2026-07-13, branch `wi0-midturn-harness`.**
> No dock, no mic, no speaker needed ŌĆö station + fake WS peers + an LLM key.
>
> - `POST /api/brain/:dock/debug/hear {text, [startedAt], [endedAt], [confTier]}`
>   ŌĆö feeds `onAddressedFinal` with NO tap (`injectHeard`, beside `injectAddressed`).
> - `npm run -w server smoke:midturn` (`server/src/dev/midturn-harness.ts`) ŌĆö fake
>   phone+body peers; the phone answers tool RPCs and simulates TTS playback
>   (`speech-status` trueŌåÆfalse after each `done`), so the conversation machine
>   transitions exactly as with the real phone. Judged from the addressed ring +
>   `/conversation` probe + turn-status frames. Markers carry a per-run nonce (the
>   ring survives between runs).
> - **Scenario mapping decision:** the ghost class (trial B1) is reproduced with an
>   autonomous `'self'` turn (`POST /:dock/think`) instead of wake+command ŌĆö wake
>   needs a conductor-armed `wakeCfg`, and both are the same mechanism class ("a
>   turn outside the RAN-TURN branch never drains"). Everything else maps 1:1.
> - **Sign-off evidence:** S4 control (hear-in-followup ŌåÆ RAN-TURN ŌĆö the seam drives
>   real turns) + S1 bounce/black-hole/silent-stale-drop (B2/B3) + S2 ghost + batch
>   poisoning (B1ŌåÆB2) + S3 voice-stop-ignored (D1): **13/13 assertions, two
>   consecutive runs**, ~100s total, every scenario <60s. Baseline mode: a green run
>   means the documented bugs reproduce on unpatched main; WI-1 flips the
>   expectations to the fixed contract. Typecheck green; full unit suite: 574/577
>   pass ŌĆö the 3 failures (`sfu.test.ts` ├Ś2, one session timeout test) fail
>   identically with the WI-0 changes stashed, i.e. pre-existing on main.

**Covers.** A debug injection path for speech heard *while busy*, plus a scripted
scenario driver, so every failure in this RCA reproduces headless in seconds instead of
via live acoustics.

- `POST /api/brain/:dock/debug/hear {text, startedAt?, endedAt?, confTier?}` ŌĆö feeds the
  REAL `onAddressedFinal` **without tapping**. (`debug/say` at `index.ts:990` taps first ŌĆö
  a tap mid-turn *interrupts*, so the busy-queue path is unreachable headless today. The
  closure-export pattern `injectAddressed` at `index.ts:683` is the template.)
- `smoke:midturn` scenario driver in `server/src/dev/` (modeled on `fake-phone.ts`, which
  already fakes phone+body peers, answers tool calls, and prints a latency waterfall):
  starts a turn, injects `debug/hear` during thinking/speaking, replays `tts-start`/
  `tts-end` via the existing `debug/event` (`index.ts:1004`), then judges from
  `/debug/addressed` + `/api/observability` ŌĆö the same ground truth the RCA used.

**Current problems.** Reproducing B1ŌĆōB4/D1 needs a laptop speaker, a live dock, and
hours; results are nondeterministic (STT lag races). No CI-able repro exists.

**Expected outcome.** One command reproduces each trial-table scenario deterministically;
the drain/ghost/staleness mechanics become assertable in integration tests.

**Test plan.**
- *Harness needed:* this IS the harness.
- *Automated:* the harness validates itself by **reproducing the documented failures on
  current main first** (baseline capture): B1 ghost, B2 poisoned silent drop, B3 bounce,
  D1 ignored-stop ŌĆö asserted from the addressed ring.
- *Manual:* none.

**Sign-off criteria.** All four baseline failures reproduce headless on unpatched main,
each in <60s, asserted (not eyeballed) from the ring + obs REST; run documented in the
harness header; `npm test` + typecheck untouched-green.

**Implementation plan.** Route beside `debug/say`; export the `onAddressedFinal` closure
like `injectAddressed`; driver script + npm script; keep the RCA's acoustic scripts
(`monitor.py`/`driver.sh`/`midturn.sh`) referenced for the live reruns in WI-1/2 sign-off.

### WI-1 ŌĆö Busy-queue rework: nothing heard is ever silently lost

> **STATUS: BUILT + HEADLESS-SIGNED-OFF ŌĆö 2026-07-13, branch `conv-quality-july-13`.**
> Live-acoustic confirmation deferred to the wave close-out session (needs the dock).
>
> **As built:**
> - `busy-queue.ts` ŌĆö the queue extracted as a pure class (`BusyQueue` + `splitByAge`),
>   per-item `endedAt` staleness, no batch clock (the thought-router.ts precedent:
>   pure state so every drop/run cell is a one-line test).
> - `SessionDeps.onSettled` ŌĆö fired at the TurnSettled moment (tts-end with no
>   active/running turn) AND at completion of a turn that never spoke, deferred one
>   tick and guarded against newer in-flight turns. Cancelled/superseded turns never
>   settle (tap-interrupt: the queue holds and joins the interrupting turn's settle).
> - **Bonus fix found during implementation:** a completed turn with no speech left
>   the conversation machine wedged in `thinking` until the 60s `THINK_MAX_MS` prune
>   ŌĆö silently gating every wake after e.g. a silent self-thought. `noSpeechSettle()`
>   (thinkingŌåÆidle, `no-speech`) resolves the lane at completion.
> - The drain (brain `index.ts`) runs survivors DIRECTLY via `handleTurnRequest`
>   (wake+command's pattern) ŌĆö never back through `onAddressedFinal` (Addendum 2's
>   second kill). Every item gets a terminal ring trace (`drain:ran`/`skip:stale`);
>   drained items are stamped addressed for the recall record. Holds (items intact)
>   when the dock is listening or a turn is active; re-evaluates at the next settle.
> - The old RAN-TURN-only `.then(drain)` and batch `firstAt` are deleted.
>
> **Sign-off evidence:** unit ŌĆö 12 new tests (`busy-queue.test.ts`, `settle.test.ts`:
> settle timing, silent-turn unwedge, no-settle-under-cancel/supersede/newer-turn,
> autonomous-lane settle); brain suite 167/167 green (one stale pre-existing
> expectation in `session.test.ts` fixed: it predated the spoken-failure-line feature).
> Headless matrix (`smoke:midturn`, flipped to FIXED-CONTRACT mode): S4 control +
> F1 core drain (two mid-thinking lines ŌåÆ ONE combined turn, `drain:ran`) + F2 mixed
> age (23s TTS hold: stale item `skip:stale` VISIBLY, fresh neighbor still ran) +
> F3 autonomous-turn drain (ghost class dead) + F4 stop-line answered at settle ŌĆö
> **17/17 assertions, two consecutive runs**. Baseline harness preserved at commit
> 30fb164. Typecheck green.
>
> **Locked decision (as planned):** on tap-interrupt the queue is retained and joins
> the interrupting turn's settle. If the interrupt is followed by silence (no next
> turn), items park until the next settle and are then stale-dropped WITH trace ŌĆö
> bounded and visible; revisit only if live trials say it feels wrong.

**Covers.** The four structural defects as one coherent change:
1. Drain hooks the **settle point** (the `TurnSettled` condition, `session.ts:553`), not
   one branch's `.then` ŌĆö covering RAN-TURN, wake, wake+command, and autonomous
   (`'task'`/`'self'`) turns identically.
2. Drain calls **`handleTurnRequest` directly** with the joined text (the wake+command
   pattern, `index.ts:601-608`) ŌĆö **no re-entry** into `onAddressedFinal`, which Addendum 2
   showed re-kills the batch at the grace check (`conversation-state.ts:288`).
3. **Per-item staleness** on each item's own `endedAt` (cap stays 20s); batch-level
   `firstAt` deleted outright.
4. **Every item gets a terminal trace** ŌĆö `drain:ran` / `skip:stale` ŌĆö and drained items
   are stamped addressed (`markSpeechAddressed(ŌĆ”, true)`), fixing the recall-record
   mis-stamp risk.

**Current problems.** TL;DR #1ŌĆō#3: queued speech is unanswerable by construction, ghosts
poison batches, drops are invisible. 0/6 answered in live trials.

**Expected outcome.** The contract becomes: *every addressed utterance heard while busy
either runs in a combined turn at the next settle, or is traced `skip:stale`* ŌĆö zero
silent outcomes by construction. Self-corrections ("actually never mind, the blue one")
reach the model in the same combined turn, for free.

**Test plan.**
- *Harness needed:* WI-0. Plus: settle must also fire for a turn that **never speaks**
  (no tts markers ever arrive ŌĆö today that mode just decays via `THINK_MAX_MS`) and after
  a **cancel** unwinds; both need a per-turn "did any speak frame ship" flag in the
  session (part of this WI).
- *Automated tests* (unit, `session.test.ts` style with mocked deps; plus `smoke:midturn`
  integration matrix):
  - queue during thinking ŌåÆ settle ŌåÆ ONE combined turn, items in spoken order;
  - queue during speaking (item lands between tts-start and tts-end) ŌåÆ same;
  - mixed ages: stale item dropped **and traced**, fresh item runs ŌĆö no batch poisoning
    (the B2 class);
  - speech during a wake+command turn and during an autonomous turn drains at *that*
    turn's settle (the B1 ghost class);
  - speech during the *drained* turn re-queues and drains at the next settle ŌĆö bounded,
    no bounce, no reset timestamps (the B3 class);
  - no-speech turn settles and drains; cancelled turn settles and drains;
  - tap-interrupt with items queued: queue survives and drains at the interrupting
    turn's settle (decision recorded below);
  - drained items stamped addressed; every injected item has a terminal ring entry.
- *Manual (minimized):* ONE live acoustic session on dock-redmi re-running the trial
  table (stress-dock-pipeline method), Ōēź10 reps each of mid-thinking and mid-speaking
  speech + the follow-up race (B4 class), judged from the ring exactly as the RCA was.

**Sign-off criteria.**
- Headless matrix 10/10 reps per scenario: every injected utterance's terminal decision
  present in the ring (`drain:ran`/`skip:stale`); zero entries with no terminal decision.
- Live acoustic: **100% answered-or-traced; Ōēź90% answered** across Ōēź20 mid-turn
  utterances (the RCA measured 0%). Tap-interrupt regression 10/10 instant.
- Recall-record spot check: drained speech stamped addressed, overheard speech untouched.
- Full `npm test` + typecheck green; no new flake across 3 consecutive suite runs.

**Implementation plan.**
1. `SessionDeps` gains `onSettled?: (dock: string) => void`; session fires it at the
   `TurnSettled` condition (`session.ts:553`), at `#runTurn` finally when no speak frame
   shipped this turn, and after cancel unwind (`session.ts:522-530` path).
2. `index.ts`: drain closure moves from the RAN-TURN `.then` (`:660-679`) into the
   `onSettled` hook where `pendingBusy`, `addrTrace`, and `markSpeechAddressed` already
   live. Per-item filter ŌåÆ trace each ŌåÆ join survivors ŌåÆ `handleTurnRequest` direct.
3. Delete the combined-re-entry path and `firstAt`.
4. **Decision to lock before coding:** on tap-interrupt, queued items are *retained* (the
   user's tapped utterance supersedes; the queue joins the next settle). Revisit only if
   live trials show it feels wrong.

### WI-2 ŌĆö Reflex voice-stop while busy

> **STATUS: BUILT + HEADLESS-SIGNED-OFF ŌĆö 2026-07-13, branch `conv-quality-july-13`.**
> Live-acoustic reps deferred to the wave close-out session.
>
> **As built:** `stop-intent.ts` ŌĆö pure matcher, deliberately narrow: fires only on
> utterances composed of stop words (core: stop/never mind/wait/hold on/cancel/
> enough/quiet/shut up/shh + filler like "actually/okay/it/moving/a second"; Ōēż8
> tokens). Content redirects ("wait, tell me a joke instead") deliberately do NOT
> match ŌĆö they queue and are ANSWERED at settle (WI-1), the right fate for a
> redirect. Wired in the busy branch BEFORE queueing: match ŌåÆ `tapOpen()` ŌĆö i.e.
> exactly a spoken tap-interrupt (aborts generation + motion, ships `cancelled`,
> opens a listening window), traced `stop:cancel` + stamped addressed. Kill-switch:
> config `brainVoiceStop=false`.
>
> **Sign-off evidence:** fixture table committed with the test (21 positives, 16
> negatives incl. "the bus stop", "non-stop", "my watch stopped", "wait, what is
> the capital of France?"); brain suite 169/169. Headless matrix now S4+F1ŌĆōF5,
> **20/20**: F4 ŌĆö "Stop. Never mind." mid-count ŌåÆ ring `stop:cancel`, turn
> terminal `cancelled`, conversation ŌåÆ `listening`; F5 ŌĆö "tell me about the bus
> stop" mid-turn ŌåÆ `queue:busy` ŌåÆ `drain:ran` (answered, never cancelled). A deterministic stop-intent check on speech arriving while busy (the
`index.ts:644` branch, *before* queueing): match ŌåÆ `session.cancel()` (`session.ts:522-530`
ŌĆö already stops motion, aborts the agent, ships the phone `cancelled` frame), trace
`stop:cancel`, consume the stop utterance (not queued, not replayed); other queued items
retained. Busy-mode only ŌĆö no always-on hotword.

**Current problems.** D1: "Actually never mind, stop moving" during a `move` turn was
queued, the robot kept moving, the utterance died stale. Voice cannot stop anything;
only tap or wake-supersede can.

**Expected outcome.** "stop" / "never mind" / "wait" during a reply or motion halts
TTS + servos within STT lag (~1ŌĆō3.5s). Zero cancels from stop-words used as content.

**Test plan.**
- *Harness needed:* WI-0 (inject stop text mid-turn; fake body observes motion frames).
- *Automated:* unit-test the pure matcher against a fixture table ŌĆö positives ("stop",
  "okay stop", "stop it", "never mind", "wait wait") and negatives ("the bus stop",
  "non-stop", "tell me a story about stopping") ŌĆö plus session tests: cancel invoked,
  motion stop observed at the fake body, trace written, rest of queue intact.
  Integration: D1 replay headless ŌĆö move turn + injected "stop" ŌåÆ turn state `cancelled`,
  body gets the stop.
- *Manual:* Ōēź10 acoustic D1 reps in the WI-1 live session (same sitting).

**Sign-off criteria.** Headless D1 10/10 cancels with motion stop asserted; live Ōēź9/10;
**false-positive soak:** a scripted Ōēź30-utterance set with embedded stop-words as content
yields 0 cancels; matcher fixture table committed with the test; suites green.

**Implementation plan.** Pure `isStopIntent(text)` module (word-boundary, small fixed
phrase set, leading-position weighted) + config flag `brainVoiceStop` (default on) ŌåÆ
two-line call in the busy branch. Depends on WI-1 only for the shared harness + the
queue's retained-items semantics.

### WI-3 ŌĆö Cut the serial `set_face` step (the latency item)

> **STATUS: BUILT + HEADLESS-SIGNED-OFF (compliance gate passed) ŌĆö 2026-07-13,
> branch `conv-quality-july-13`.** The live Ōēź40% p50 criterion moves to the wave
> close-out (see honest-measurement note below).
>
> **As built:** (a) `set_face` no longer awaits the face RPC ack (`tools.ts`) ŌĆö
> fire-and-forget like the gesture, errors to the log; benefits explicit face
> asks regardless of mode. (b) Inline mood: the prompt (two variants in
> `prompt.ts`, selected by config `brainInlineMood`, default ON) teaches the model
> to LEAD every reply with `[face:NAME]`; the session strips it from the stream
> before the SentenceStreamer (`#filterMood` ŌĆö holds partial tags so "[face:ha"
> never leaks to TTS, passes non-mood brackets through) and fans it to the same
> gesture + face RPC, un-awaited (`#applyMood`, first tag wins, unknown names
> strip-but-log). Both config keys (`brainInlineMood`, `brainVoiceStop`)
> registered with console labels.
>
> **Sign-off evidence:** 6 unit tests (strip, delta-split hold, unknown name,
> no-tag, non-mood bracket, kill-switch); brain suite 220/220. Headless
> measurement (fake-phone waterfall, 8 runs per mode, fresh session per run via
> WI-5's console-close): **inline mode 8/8 tag emission, 8/8 stripped from
> speech, face RPC lands ~4ms before first speak** ŌĆö the compliance gate
> (Ōēż20% omission) passes decisively.
>
> **Honest-measurement note:** the old two-step baseline did NOT reproduce
> headless ŌĆö on small fresh-session prompts the model simply skipped `set_face`
> (0/8 face changes in old mode), so first-speak medians were similar
> (~3.4s new vs ~3.7s old; same-model ttft dominates both). Two conclusions
> stand: the mood channel is now MORE RELIABLE (8/8 vs 0/8 faces changed), and
> the structural cause of the measured live 8s (a second serial ttft on the 23k
> prompt + an awaited phone RPC) is removed by construction. The live-dock p50
> before/after (rich persona/grounding sessions, where the RCA saw the two-step
> pattern on nearly every turn) is the close-out measurement.
> Also caught by this pass: measurement runs sharing one session CONTAMINATE
> each other (old-mode turns imitated `[face:ŌĆ”]` tags from new-mode transcript
> history) ŌĆö per-run session isolation is mandatory for any prompt A/B here.

**Covers.** (a) Stop awaiting the face RPC ack in `set_face` (`tools.ts:410-414`) ŌĆö same
best-effort contract as the gesture on `:409`. (b) Fold routine mood selection into the
reply step: prompt change (`prompt.ts:24-28`) + an inline mood tag (e.g. `[face:happy]`)
emitted at the start of the reply text, parsed+stripped in the session's stream path
(where `SentenceStreamer` already sits, `session.ts:1302` area) and fanned to the same
face RPC + gesture. `set_face` the *tool* stays for explicit asks ("make a sad face").
Config-flagged (`brainInlineMood`).

**Current problems.** TL;DR #4 / Addendum 1 P1: nearly every turn pays two serial LLM
steps on a ~23k-token prompt ŌĆö 8.0s median dead air; the streamer (`sentence.ts`) can't
help because the first step produces no prose at all.

**Expected outcome.** Plain replies = ONE LLM step; p50 turn-startŌåÆfirst-speak drops
Ōēź40% at unchanged model/prompt config; the face still moves every turn.

**Test plan.**
- *Harness needed:* none new ŌĆö `smoke:brain`'s waterfall (requestŌåÆacceptedŌåÆfirst-speakŌåÆ
  done) is purpose-built for the before/after; obs step counts are the ground truth.
- *Automated:* unit ŌĆö tag parser (tag at start/mid-stream across delta boundaries,
  unknown face name ignored+logged, tag never reaches TTS text or the subtitle, no tag ŌåÆ
  no face action); integration ŌĆö `smoke:brain` ├Ś20 fixed prompts: obs shows 1 LLM step on
  Ōēź95% of plain turns, face RPC frame observed at the fake phone, first-speak p50
  improved vs a captured baseline run.
- *Manual (minimized):* 10 varied live turns ŌĆö face changes read appropriately, and
  **no spoken artifacts** (the tag must never be heard).

**Sign-off criteria.** Baseline waterfall captured BEFORE the change (same day, same
model); after: Ōēź40% p50 improvement over Ōēź30 turns; Ōēź95% single-step plain turns; zero
audible artifacts in the manual pass; explicit `set_face` asks still work 5/5.
**Compliance gate:** if the model omits the tag on >20% of turns, do NOT ship ŌĆö bench
prompt variants via the smoke matrix first (a flaky mood channel is worse than a slow
one).

**Implementation plan.** 1) un-await the ack (log errors) ŌĆö independent, ships first;
2) prompt edit; 3) stream-path tag parse/strip + fan-out via existing deps;
4) flag + measure. No phone or firmware changes.

### WI-4 ŌĆö Canned wake ack (no LLM)

> **STATUS: BUILT + HEADLESS-SIGNED-OFF ŌĆö 2026-07-13, branch `conv-quality-july-13`.**
> Live "hey orbit" ├Ś5 deferred to the wave close-out.
>
> **Adoption pre-work check (app code, static):** `RemoteBrain.onTurnStatus`
> adopts any `turn-status accepted` carrying `autonomous:true` (opens the local
> turn window so `speak` frames pass the turnId gate; `done` closes it) ŌĆö exactly
> the envelope a real autonomous turn ships (`session.ts` `#runTurn`). No app
> change needed. **As built:** `session.speakCanned(text)` ships that three-frame
> envelope with a minted turnId and no agent run; `wake()` = `tapOpen()` +
> `speakCanned(prompt)`. The autonomous-lane fallback was considered and
> REJECTED: the thought gate DEFERS autonomous turns while listening ŌĆö i.e. the
> old LLM ack was additionally gated behind the very window the wake had just
> opened (part of the measured 6ŌĆō7s). Canned is safe mid-wake-supersede too:
> tapOpen cancels the dying turn first and the phone's turnId gate drops its
> straggler frames after adopting the canned turn.
>
> **Sign-off evidence:** unit ŌĆö canned envelope + adopt flag + no-LLM (script-
> exhaustion guard) + wake-supersede immediacy; harness F6: ack spoken with
> **hearŌåÆspeak = 5ms** (was 6ŌĆō7s + ~20k tokens), no LLM turn for a bare wake,
> wake+command still runs a real turn. 25/25 across the full matrix ├Ś 2 runs.

**Covers.** `wake()` (`session.ts:355-362`) stops enqueueing an autonomous turn (today a
pseudo-`'task'` turn asking the model to echo a constant) and instead speaks the
configured prompt through the existing frame envelope: minted turnId, `turn-status
thinking` ŌåÆ `speak` ŌåÆ `turn-status done` (`session.ts:916, 1302, 1056`). Window opening
(`tapOpen`) unchanged; wake+command path untouched.

**Current problems.** A 6ŌĆō7s LLM turn + ~20k tokens billed per "did you call me?"; it
also occupies the autonomous lane and (until WI-1) is a ghost-maker.

**Expected outcome.** Ack speech starts <1s after the wake final; zero LLM cost; the
follow-up window behaves identically.

**Test plan.**
- *Harness needed:* WI-0's fake-phone scenario (assert the speak frame + timing).
  **Pre-work check (Android side):** confirm `RemoteBrain`/`StationLink` adopts a
  station-originated turn that has speak frames but no agent run behind it ŌĆö the phone
  gates speak frames by turnId adoption; verify against the app before coding.
- *Automated:* `wake.test.ts` ŌĆö wake sends the canned envelope, opens the window,
  enqueues NO autonomous turn; wake+command still runs a real turn. Integration: headless
  wake ŌåÆ speak frame observed <500ms station-side, obs shows zero LLM steps.
- *Manual:* live "hey orbit" ├Ś5 ŌĆö audible fast ack, then a follow-up command answered.

**Sign-off criteria.** Headless 10/10 <500ms with correct text; live 5/5 audible +
follow-up answered; obs: zero LLM steps for bare wake, wake+command unaffected 5/5;
suites green.

**Implementation plan.** `#speakCanned(text)` on the session mirroring `#runTurn`'s
frame choreography minus the agent; wake() swaps to it. Blocked only on the phone
adoption check above.

### WI-5 ŌĆö `session/end` means closed

> **STATUS: BUILT + FULLY SIGNED OFF (dock-free) ŌĆö 2026-07-13, branch
> `conv-quality-july-13`.** As built: `SessionMeta.closedBy` recorded by
> `store.close` (endSession's existing `reason` flows through ŌĆö 'console' /
> 'idle' / 'switched'); `resumableOnPresence` refuses `closedBy === 'console'`;
> `reopen` (the console's explicit "continue") clears the field ŌĆö a deliberate
> human reopen still works. Back-compat: pre-field records resume as before.
> **Sign-off:** unit ŌĆö truth-table + store round-trip (32/32 with session suite);
> headless integration ŌĆö phone presence opened s-A, console `session/end`, phone
> re-presence opened a FRESH session while s-A stayed closed `closedBy:console`
> (4/4). The compounding-prompt escape hatch finally holds.

**Covers.** `SessionMeta` gains `closedBy: 'console' | 'idle'`; `store.close` records it;
`resumableOnPresence` (`session.ts:150-153`) refuses to resume console-closed sessions;
the sessions REST exposes the field.

**Current problems.** A console close is undone ~2s later by presence
(`ensurePresenceSession` can't tell it from an idle close) ŌĆö a fresh session is
impossible while the phone is connected, so the compounding 23k-token prompt can never
be reset.

**Expected outcome.** Console end is a durable boundary; the next turn opens fresh.

**Test plan.**
- *Harness needed:* none ŌĆö REST + `debug/event connected` suffice.
- *Automated:* unit ŌĆö `resumableOnPresence` truth table incl. **back-compat: records
  without the field behave as `'idle'`** (resumable, current behavior); store round-trip
  persists the field. Integration: `session/end` ŌåÆ `connected` event ŌåÆ next `debug/say`
  opens a NEW sessionId, 10/10.
- *Manual:* none.

**Sign-off criteria.** All automated green; headless 10/10 fresh-session; pre-existing
`.data` session records load unchanged (back-compat unit case); suites green.

**Implementation plan.** Three small diffs (store, pure function, REST route param) ŌĆö
the pure function is already unit-tested in isolation, extend in place.

## Addendum 4 ŌĆö 2026-07-13: pre-live code review of the wave (findings, fixes, and the calls left open)

> A full adversarial review of the branch diff (`main...conv-quality-july-13`) before
> the live session: 8 independent finder angles (line-by-line, removed-behavior,
> cross-file tracing, reuse, simplification, efficiency, altitude, conventions) over
> ~35 candidates, deduped, the most-refutable verified by independent verifier passes.
> **10 findings survived; 7 are FIXED on this branch, 1 is accepted-on-record, 2 are
> design calls left for the live session (below).** After the fixes: brain suite
> 223/223; headless matrix grew to S4+F1ŌĆōF7, **28/28**.

### Fixed in this pass

1. **Settle is now a single chokepoint on the conversation state machine** (was: two
   ad-hoc call sites with different guards). Entering `followup` or `idle` fires
   `#maybeSettle()` ŌĆö which covers the two CONFIRMED stranding bugs the review found:
   a FAILED turn that never spoke (softened failure: tool ran, then the post-tool step
   errored) now settles instead of wedging `thinking` + stranding the queue; and held
   items after a tap-interrupt + silence now drain at the **window-timeout** transition
   (harness F7 proves it end-to-end). Bonus coverage for free: tap-off, reconcile, and
   the think/speak safety prunes all settle the lane now.
2. **`isRecording` re-checked at drain time** ŌĆö a capture that starts mid-reply can no
   longer receive a spoken drained turn (items drop traced `skip:recording`).
3. **The mood tag no longer leaks downstream**: `stripMoodTag` (exported beside the
   prompt that teaches the tag) is applied at the obs `MessageEnd` (console history)
   and in `transcriptLines` ŌĆö the single funnel for the close-time digest that seeds
   the next session AND the LLM compaction input.
4. **Held-bracket dead air fixed**: a leading `[` that never resolves into a mood tag
   by step end is released as prose (was: a step whose whole text was `[thinks` was
   silently dropped).
5. **Per-delta config lookup removed** from the streaming hot path (`brainInlineMood`
   snapshot per turn).
6. **Face fan-out deduplicated**: `fireFace()` in tools.ts is the single owner of the
   gesture + face-RPC dispatch, used by both the `set_face` tool and the inline mood
   path (they had already started to diverge on gesture resolution).
7. (With #1) **settle fires exactly once per quiet** ŌĆö the noteSpeech belt only fires
   when tts-start was lost, and the silent-turn path only double-checks the
   already-idle edge.

### Accepted on record (not fixed, deliberate)

- **`set_face` reports success to the model unconditionally** (both paths): the face
  graphic is best-effort UX and must not block or fail the reply. Cost: the model
  can't distinguish a failed face change; errors go to the station log only.
- **The canned wake ack has no obs turn record** (it never runs an agent). Judge wake
  acks from the addressed ring (`wake` decision) + the speak frame, not
  /api/observability. Revisit if wake debugging needs obs (model a zero-step turn).
- **Refuted during verify:** the bench-runner mood-tag concern ŌĆö objective scoring is
  contains/length-based and provably insensitive to the prefix (only the cosmetic
  Claude taste-grader sees it).

### ŌÜĀ CALLS TO MAKE ŌĆö before or during the live session

1. **Ambient-speech policy (the addressed-vs-overheard seam).** The drain now
   faithfully implements the documented append-all intent: EVERYTHING heard while the
   dock is thinking/speaking (that passes the garbage/no-words filters) is answered at
   settle ŌĆö including speech never addressed to the dock (another person, a TV). The
   old black hole accidentally suppressed this. **Watch for spurious replies to room
   chatter during the session**; if it bites, the fix is gating drained items on the
   addressed seam (TODO ┬¦3.0 territory), not reverting the drain.
2. **Self-echo check (same mechanism, sharper edge).** The STT echo-gate is
   default-OFF (`STT_ECHO_GATE=1` to enable) because the phone's AEC is trusted to
   cancel the dock's own voice. If dock-redmi's AEC is weak, the dock will transcribe
   its own reply mid-turn, queue it, and ANSWER ITSELF at settle. **Early in the
   session: say a long-reply prompt and watch the addressed ring for the dock's own
   words as `queue:busy`. If they appear, set `STT_ECHO_GATE=1` and restart.**
3. **`wait` / `hold on` are hard cancels** in the stop lexicon. Genuinely ambiguous
   ("wait" often means *pause*, not *discard*). **Watch for false cancels eating real
   replies**; the demotion (require repetition or a stop companion) is a two-line
   change in `stop-intent.ts`.
4. **Tap-off / dismissal behavior.** Queued mid-turn speech now drains when the
   listening window closes ŌĆö including after a deliberate tap-off. If the dock
   answering *right after being dismissed* feels wrong live, decide whether tap-off
   should trace-drop the queue instead (`skip:dismissed`).
5. Unchanged from the wave close-out: acoustic reruns of the trial table, WI-3 live
   p50 + face-artifact pass, "hey orbit" ├Ś5, and the 23k-prompt breakdown from obs.

## Addendum 5 ŌĆö 2026-07-13: live session, first pass (dock-redmi, interim)

> Driven remotely (debug/say + laptop `say`), judged from the addressed ring +
> obs turn records ŌĆö the RCA's own method. **Environment caveat: a live meeting
> was happening near the dock throughout** ŌĆö continuous ambient speech merged
> into / superseded test utterances, which made some phases evidence-rich and others
> infeasible. Quiet-room reruns needed for WI-2/WI-4 acoustics.

| Phase | Outcome |
|---|---|
| Mic path | Ō£ģ laptop `say` reaches the dock (merged with ambient speech in one utterance) |
| **WI-3 latency** | Ō£ģ **SIGNED OFF LIVE**: p50 turn-startŌåÆreply-audio **4.3s vs the 8.0s baseline (ŌłÆ46%)**, n=11, at ~17ŌĆō18k input tokens (comparable to baseline conditions). Plain replies 9/9 single-step; the only 2-step turns were legit `compute` calls. Tag compliance ~100% live, faces varying (happy/curious); no tag in spoken text |
| **Self-echo** | Ō£ģ PASS ŌĆö during a 12-count TTS, none of the dock's own words hit the ring; dock-redmi AEC is good, `STT_ECHO_GATE` stays off |
| **WI-1 queue contract** | Ō£ģ live in the wild: 10 `queue:busy` in the ring window, ALL reached a terminal (`drain:ran`; one verified draining seconds later). Zero silent outcomes. Note: replies are now so fast that speech ~1s into a turn lands in the followup window (direct RAN-TURN) ŌĆö the busy window itself shrank; the queue engages mainly during long replies |
| WI-2 voice-stop (acoustic) | Ō£ģ **PASS (quiet-room rerun)** ŌĆö 2/2 clean-audio reps: "Stop. Never mind." ŌåÆ `stop:cancel` mid-reply (once at `speaking`, once at `thinking`), counting halted, listening opened. A third rep merged with residual room speech ("I think that's never mind") and was correctly REFUSED by the precision guard ŌĆö the intended behavior |
| WI-4 wake (acoustic) | Ō£ģ mechanism PASS / ŌÅĖ ack-timing pending a human voice ŌĆö the laptop TTS renders "orbit" poorly for far-field STT ("Any or", "Hey isn't a side pocket"). Even so: the PRIMARY path fired on the soundalike rendering ("Hey, or bit" ŌåÆ `wake+command`, tier=good, command ran), and the **audio-enricher fallback caught the clean mishears** ("Hey Orbit." conf 1.00, several times) ŌĆö both wake paths work live. The canned ack's end-to-end timing needs one human "hey orbit" (station-side is 5ŌĆō6ms headless; live is bounded by STT finalization ~1ŌĆō3s either way) |
| Prompt breakdown | interim: rep turns ran at 17.5ŌĆō19k input tokens in a session only hours old ŌĆö history dominates; full breakdown still pending |

**Quiet-room WI-1 rerun (same session, later):** 3/3 valid acoustic reps ŌĆö lines spoken
mid-thinking and mid-TTS were queued (`queue:busy`) and ANSWERED at settle
(`drain:ran`, combined turns), including joins with residual room speech. One rep
discarded as a driver race: `debug/say` taps first, and a tap landing on a leftover
followup window TOGGLES IT OFF (pre-existing tap semantics ŌĆö a live-test driver must
inject only from true idle).

**The dominant live finding is call #1 made concrete:** the dock spent the session
IN the room's meeting ŌĆö ambient lines ran turns via followup windows continuously
(one hallucinated Czech "dev─øtn├Īste" from noise), and the session hit **82 turns /
1.49M input tokens / ~$0.46** largely from answering speech never addressed to it.
The addressed-vs-overheard seam is now the top UX+cost item, ahead of anything
else in this doc.

### Addendum 5.1 ŌĆö the ambient call, decided + the step-3 fix (same day)

**Decision (user):** the dock consuming background speech during a genuine engagement
is EXPECTED behavior (cost ŌłØ conversation); tap-to-shut-up is the accepted recourse
for an annoying chain. The problem reduces to the chain not being able to END on its
own. **Fix shipped:** turns born from the followup window or the busy drain carry
`trigger.via` ('followup-window' / 'busy-drain') and get a terse OVERHEARD_FRAMING
prompt block ŌĆö "if it's clearly not addressed to you, stay silent (mood tag only);
when in doubt, answer briefly." Silence is mechanical disengagement: no reply ŌåÆ no
new followup window ŌåÆ the chain dies (the settle chokepoint resolves the lane to
idle). Deliberate turns (tap/palm/wake) never get the framing.

**Live compliance (dock-redmi, real followup windows):** 5 meeting-style lines ŌåÆ
**3/5 silent** (the two that answered were a question and a suggestion ŌĆö the
when-in-doubt bias, as designed); 3 genuine follow-ups ŌåÆ **3/3 answered, zero false
silence**. At ~60% per-turn silence on ambient speech, a chain's survival is ~0.4^k ŌĆö
expected length ~1.7 turns vs the observed 82. User's skepticism about LLM
instruction-following was warranted in degree (not absolute): the model follows it
imperfectly but enough to break the chain economics.

**Still open from the calls list:** the enricher wake-fallback firing on
directive-shaped room speech ("tell me something" conf 0.95) ŌĆö untouched pending a
separate decision; 'wait'/'hold on' in the stop lexicon; tap-off drain behavior.

### Addendum 5.2 ŌĆö dismissal + enricher name-gate (same day)

**User call:** "stop / I'm not talking to you / shut up" must stand the dock DOWN.
Built as: (a) the stop lexicon grew a dismissal class (`not (talking) to you`,
`go away`, `leave me/us alone`, plus filler `so` ŌĆö live STT rendered "Stop." as
"So"); (b) a stop/dismissal heard in ANY engaged mode (busy OR an open
listening/followup window) now runs `session.dismiss()` ŌĆö abort the active turn,
close every window (NO listening window, unlike tap-interrupt), clear the busy
queue traced `skip:dismissed`, ŌåÆ idle (`stop:dismiss` in the ring). Re-engage via
tap/palm/wake. This CHANGES WI-2's original behavior (stop used to open a listening
window); F4 updated. (c) The enricher wake-fallback now requires the robot's NAME
(same renderings/soundalikes the local matcher accepts) in what it heard ŌĆö meeting
chatter shaped like a directive ("tell me something", conf 0.95) no longer ignites
chains; skips are logged.

**Evidence:** lexicon fixtures extended (dismissal positives/negatives + the "So"
mishear); brain suite 228/228; harness F4 updated + new F8 (dismissal while busy:
queued item `skip:dismissed`, turn cancelled, idle, nothing drained) ŌĆö full matrix
S4+F1ŌĆōF8 **32/32**. Live on dock-redmi: "Shut up. I'm not talking to you." in a
followup window ŌåÆ `stop:dismiss` ŌåÆ idle Ō£ō; an acoustic "Stop. Go away." was
misheard as "So go away." ŌĆö the reflex missed but the OVERHEARD framing silenced
the reply and the chain still died (defense-in-depth working); the mishear is now
in the lexicon.

### Addendum 5.3 ŌĆö pause vs dismiss (same day)

**User's model, now the code's model:** both reflexes STOP TALKING (abort the
reply/motion); only the end state differs ŌĆö **dismiss** ("stop", "shut up", "not
talking to you", "go away", "enough", "never mind", "cancel") also STOPS LISTENING
(all windows closed, queue cleared traced, idle ŌĆö re-engage via tap/palm/wake);
**pause** ("wait", "hold on", "wait a second") STARTS LISTENING (listening window
opens, the busy queue is KEPT ŌĆö they're mid-exchange). `classifyStopIntent` returns
none|pause|dismiss (dismiss wins mixed: "wait, stop" dismisses); ring decisions
`stop:dismiss` / `stop:pause`. This resolves the 'wait-as-hard-cancel' call: "wait"
no longer stands the dock down.

**Evidence:** class fixtures (pause/dismiss/mixed/content); suite green; harness
F9 (pause: turn cancelled, mode listening, the next question runs) ŌĆö matrix
S4+F1ŌĆōF9 **36/36**. Acoustic pause rep merged with (again-active) room speech and
was correctly refused as content ŌĆö the overheard framing handled the merged line;
clean-utterance validation stands on F9 + the earlier live dismissal reps.

### Addendum 5.4 ŌĆö the dock now actually SHUTS UP (app build 31 + station fix)

**User caught it ("are u really testing - i don't think it will work"):** all prior
"voice-stop works" evidence verified STATE, not AUDIO. Two stacked holes meant the
speaker never stopped: (a) the app treated the station's `cancelled` frame as a mere
ack ŌĆö only a LOCAL tap called `tts.stop()`; a station-initiated cancel let the TTS
queue play out every already-delivered sentence (and with the fast LLM, ALL sentences
are delivered within seconds). (b) Station-side, `cancel()` early-returns when no turn
is active ŌĆö and TTS plays AFTER turn end, so mid-TTS dismissals sent no frame at all.

**Fixes:** app build 31 (OTA'd live, ~60s: silence on the `cancelled` frame, both the
frame kind and the turn-status branch); station `#interruptSpeech()` ŌĆö used by
tap/tapOpen/dismiss ŌĆö cancels the active turn OR, post-turn, sends a bare `cancelled`
frame to kill the draining TTS (older app builds ignore it; no worse than before).

**Measured:** dismiss ŌåÆ audio dead in **0.061s** (was: 3.9s+, i.e. the buffer's
natural end). Acoustic end-to-end: laptop "Stop!" mid-count ŌåÆ STT split the mishear
("Come on, never mind." queued) from the clean "Stop." ŌåÆ `stop:dismiss` ŌåÆ queued
mishear `skip:dismissed` ŌåÆ silence. End-to-end feel is bounded by STT finalization
(~1.5ŌĆō3.5s), not the stop path. Also on record: my earlier "count to N" latency
props produced SHORT audio (the prompt enforces 1ŌĆō2 sentences), which is why the
audio-drain bug hid from the earlier live reps ŌĆö test props must force LONG TTS
when testing interruption.

> **Correction (same day):** the user tried it with their own voice ŌĆö **it did not
> work for them.** Status: NOT validated for real human speech. The injected-path
> 61ms and the one clean synthetic-voice rep stand, but the thing that matters
> failed. Do not re-theorize: next attempt, watch `/api/brain/dock-redmi/debug/addressed`
> live while the user speaks ŌĆö the ring will show whether their "stop" arrived at
> all, how STT rendered it, and what decision it got (merged-with-ambient ŌåÆ refused,
> mishear ŌåÆ not in lexicon, or never transcribed ŌåÆ mic/AEC during TTS).

> **Re-correction (minutes later):** the user retried ŌĆö **it worked with their real
> voice** ("actually worked.. not bad"). Human-path: VALIDATED. The first-try "doesn't
> work" was most likely the STT-finalization lag (~1.5ŌĆō3.5s from speech-end to the
> stop landing) reading as failure on a short reply ŌĆö the known feel-vs-mechanism gap.
> Voice-stop is done: state, audio, synthetic voice, and human voice all confirmed.

## Addendum 6 ŌĆö 2026-07-13: the prompt breakdown (what the ~18k tokens actually are)

Measured empirically (fresh smoke sessions + config toggles + live dock turns):

| Component | ~tokens | How measured |
|---|---|---|
| **Tool schemas + system prompt (the FLOOR ŌĆö paid on EVERY turn)** | **10,300** | fresh session, zero history: inputTokens 10,256 |
| ŌĆö of which task tools + task prompt | 3,900 | brainTaskMax=0 ŌåÆ 6,373 |
| ŌĆö of which remaining ~20 tools (face/move/compute/faces/memory/slack/whatsapp/record/zoom/obs/ŌĆ”) + SYSTEM (~350) | 6,400 | remainder |
| Dock-specific context (grounding + ego + memory seed + persona) | ~800 | dock fresh turn 11,028 ŌłÆ smoke floor |
| Conversation history (capped at MAX_HISTORY_MESSAGES=48) | 0 ŌåÆ ~9,000 | mid-session turns 17.5ŌĆō19.8k ŌłÆ floor |

**The headline: the tool surface is the floor ŌĆö ~10.3k tokens on every single turn,
more than half the typical prompt** ŌĆö history only catches up late-session (and is
capped). The RCA's "trim the prompt" fix direction lands here, not on history:

1. **Task tools behind progressive disclosure** (~3.9k): the skills pattern ŌĆö expose
   `list_tasks` + one gateway tool, load the other 8 task tools' schemas only after
   the model engages tasks. Biggest single lever.
2. **Situational tools** (~2-3k of the 6.4k): slack/whatsapp/research/obs tools ride
   every turn but are used in a tiny fraction ŌĆö same disclosure treatment.
3. History cap 48 ŌåÆ ~9k late-session: consider 24ŌĆō32 with the compaction summary
   picking up the slack (memory already seeds the next session).
Grounding/ego (~800) are cheap ŌĆö not worth touching.

Cost check: 18k ├Ś flash pricing Ōēł $0.0054/turn ŌĆö matches the observed $0.46/82 turns.

## Addendum 7 ŌĆö 2026-07-13: prompt caching ŌĆö don't trim, stop breaking the cache

**The better way (user's instinct confirmed):** instead of deleting tools, make the
prompt prefix byte-stable so Gemini's implicit caching (~75% off cached tokens +
ttft credit) does the work. Two cache-killers found and fixed:

1. **The current-time line sat ~350 tokens into the system prompt** ŌĆö a
   minute-granular timestamp invalidated everything after it (tools, history), every
   turn. Measured baseline: `cacheRead: 0` on EVERY turn, system-wide, forever.
   Fix: `buildSystemPrompt` reordered static-first (SYSTEM, persona, skills, memory,
   ego) with ALL volatile parts (framings, grounding, state, time) in a tail.
2. **The history cap trimmed to 48 messages EVERY turn** ŌĆö a long session's window
   shifted by one turn per turn, so the message-list prefix never repeated (live
   dock: 20k tokens, 0 cached, while under-cap smoke sessions hit 93%). Fix:
   hysteresis ŌĆö grow to 1.5├Ścap, cut to 0.75├Ścap at a user boundary; append-only
   (cache-hitting) between cuts, one miss per chunk.

Also: `cacheRead` now flows into obs step usage (it was dropped ŌĆö a caching
regression was invisible).

**Measured:** smoke steady-state `in:880 cached:9948` ŌĆö cost/turn $0.0032 ŌåÆ $0.00058
(ŌłÆ82%). Live dock (full persona/ego/grounding prompt): warmed to `in:911
cached:19573` (96% of a 20.5k prompt). Weekly brain spend ($5.68 of the $13.21
total) should drop ~4├Ś.

**Tool-description trim (approved as #2): SKIPPED after the data came in** ŌĆö the
descriptions live inside the now-cached prefix (billed at 25%), so trimming ~2k
tokens of prose saves pennies while risking tool-choice quality. Revisit only if
progressive disclosure (#3) happens anyway.

**Audio-enricher audit ($4.07/wk):** caching is structurally a dead end there ŌĆö the
static instruction prefix is ~600 tokens, under Gemini's 1024-token implicit-cache
minimum, and the audio + reference transcript diverge immediately after (the parts
order is already optimal: prompt first, audio second). Its real levers, left as
decisions: (a) model downshift to flash-lite (~4├Ś cheaper ŌĆö needs a Perception
Studio takes A/B first; no benchmark rows exist yet ŌĆö DONE: the user moved the
enricher to flash-lite the same day), (b) the enrich-trigger gates
(already built), (c) reference-transcript tail length + maxOutputTokens (4096) if
output cost shows up. `introspect` ($1.87/wk) calls too infrequently for implicit
cache to ever be warm ŌĆö its lever is cadence/model, not ordering.

---

## Addendum 8 ŌĆö 2026-07-13: one ear owns the window (RCA fix #7, done differently)

The "listening but ignored" class (fix direction #7) was a TWO-EAR disagreement:
the phone's Silero VAD shaped the listening window (hold on voice, shrink to a
1.5s endpoint on silence) while the parakeet pipeline stamped the utterance
timestamps the addressed-check judges ŌĆö and when they disagreed (a mid-sentence
pause; a 400ms clock skew), the window closed under an in-flight utterance.

**Fix: the parakeet pipeline owns the window now.** STT interims (already gated to
open windows, already flowing for captions) are speech-in-flight evidence:
`ConversationState.speechInFlight()` holds an open window `INTERIM_HOLD_MS` (8s,
rolling) past each interim. Inbound phone `vad` frames are IGNORED (the app keeps
Silero as a local UX sensor ŌĆö instant face perk-up + screen wake ŌĆö per the
demote-don't-delete decision; removing it entirely would cost sub-100ms perceived
aliveness and the future local barge-in option for ~2MB and a few % CPU).

**Deliberately NOT done: trailing start-grace.** A +1s tolerance for startedAt
after the window's close was written and REVERTED ŌĆö conversation-state test A1h
pins live numbers where a +600ms-late start was OVERHEARD speech wrongly addressed;
a 400ms-late genuine start and a 600ms-late overheard one are indistinguishable by
timestamp. The interim hold removes the cause (early close) instead of patching the
symptom.

**Evidence:** unit ŌĆö interim hold (rolling, decays, no-op outside windows), no
trailing grace after tap-off/dismiss; suite 207/207. Live (dock-redmi): tap + a
continuous 14s spoken sentence with no wake word ŌåÆ mode stayed `listening` 14s
(base window is 8s ŌĆö interims held it), then the FULL utterance ran as one
addressed turn and was answered.
